title: ericameria brachylepis
intro: Ericameria brachylepis, also known as a shrub or dwarf golden bush, is a small shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its golden yellow flowers and silvery-gray foliage. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties. It is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, relieving digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. Ericameria brachylepis can be prepared into herbal remedies, including teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat various ailments.
